skill.name: learn-reset
skill.description: Clear the knowledge base and start fresh
skill.summary: Clear the knowledge base and start fresh
skill.author: 0xrdan

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives from Markdown inline code and fenced examples. The weak cryptography findings are also false positives; no cryptographic algorithm use was found. The real risk is intentional local data clearing of knowledge files and state after user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Intentional Knowledge File Clearing
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to clear accumulated knowledge files, classification cache, session context, and state data. It requires user confirmation and preserves directory structure, but the action can cause irreversible local data loss.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes clearing knowledge files and resetting state, and it warns that the action cannot be undone. The confirmation requirement reduces malicious concern but does not remove data-loss risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive Backtick Execution Findings
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code and fenced example blocks for file paths, YAML, JSON, and output formatting. No executable Ruby or shell backtick expression was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are documentation examples, not code that can execute. No script file, command invocation, or user-controlled shell construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Findings
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are descriptive text and placeholders about clearing a knowledge base. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain normal documentation words or template placeholders. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or configuration.

content.value_statement: Accumulated learning data can become stale, noisy, or inappropriate for a new project phase. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a confirmed reset of knowledge files, cache, session context, and state.
actual_capabilities: Asks for user confirmation before resetting stored knowledge.
Clears documented learning files for patterns, quirks, and decisions.
Clears classification cache and session context files.
Resets learning state values to their initial settings.
Reports counts for cleared knowledge categories after completion.
limitations: It can remove useful accumulated project knowledge.
It does not remove old knowledge from git history.
It depends on the expected knowledge directory layout.
It does not create backups before clearing files.

use_cases.title: Start a New Project Phase
use_cases.description: Reset stored assumptions before the assistant learns from a changed codebase or workflow.

use_cases.title: Remove Stale Learning Data
use_cases.description: Clear old patterns, quirks, and decisions that no longer match the current repository.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Clean Demo Workspace
use_cases.description: Return the knowledge base to an empty state before sharing or demonstrating the setup.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Reset
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a clean knowledge base.
prompt_templates.prompt: Reset the learned knowledge for this workspace. Ask me to confirm before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Reset With Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a clear report of what changed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Clear the learning files, cache, session context, and state. After I confirm, summarize each category that was reset.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Before Reset
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want to inspect the impact first.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check what knowledge data would be cleared, show me the affected categories, and wait for confirmation before resetting anything.

prompt_templates.title: Controlled Maintenance Reset
prompt_templates.scenario: Use during repository maintenance or onboarding preparation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a cautious knowledge reset for this repository. Confirm the expected files exist, ask for approval, reset them, then report remaining limitations.

output_examples.output: The assistant asks for confirmation, then reports that patterns, quirks, decisions, cache entries, and learning state were reset.

output_examples.input: Show me what would be cleared before reset.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the learning files, cache, session context, and state file that are expected to change.
The assistant confirms completion and notes that learning mode is disabled after the reset.

best_practices: Review the affected files before confirming a reset.
Commit or back up valuable knowledge before clearing it.
Use the reset when stored learning conflicts with current project reality.
anti_patterns: Do not reset knowledge when you only need to remove one outdated entry.
Do not run it without understanding that the action is destructive.
Do not rely on it to remove knowledge from version control history.

faq.question: Does this skill delete directories?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that it preserves the knowledge directory structure.

faq.question: Does it ask before clearing files?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions require user confirmation before resetting knowledge.

faq.question: Can the reset be undone?
faq.answer: No automatic undo is provided. Use backups or git history if recovery is needed.

faq.question: What data does it reset?
faq.answer: It resets learning files, classification cache, session context, and learning state.

faq.question: Does it remove old knowledge from git history?
faq.answer: No. The notes state that git history may still contain old knowledge.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for shared repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Confirm the reset scope and preserve important shared knowledge before running it.
